lesson.title: Row Reduction & RREF
lesson.body: Row reduction applies three elementary operations — swapping rows, scaling a row by a nonzero scalar, and adding a multiple of one row to another — without changing the solution set. Reduced Row Echelon Form (RREF) requires each pivot to be 1 with zeros above and below it. Pivot positions correspond to basic variables; columns without pivots yield free variables. The system Ax = b is consistent iff b is not a pivot column in the augmented matrix.

rooms.question: Which operation is NOT a valid elementary row operation?
steps: Valid operations: row swap, scale a row, add a multiple of one row to another.
answers: Multiply two rows together
Swap two rows
Multiply a row by a nonzero scalar
Add a multiple of one row to another row
rooms.explanation: Multiplying two rows together is not an elementary row operation. The three valid operations are: swap, scale (by nonzero), and row addition.

rooms.question: Which matrix is in Reduced Row Echelon Form (RREF)?
steps: RREF: pivots are 1, each pivot is the only nonzero entry in its column, pivots move right and down.
rooms.explanation: The first matrix has pivots 1 in columns 1 and 2, each pivot is the only nonzero in its column, and pivots step right-downward. The others violate RREF conditions.

rooms.question: The system Ax = b has infinitely many solutions when:
steps: Count pivots vs variables. Free variables exist ⇒ infinitely many solutions (if consistent).
answers: The system is consistent and has at least one free variable
The system has more equations than unknowns
Every column of A is a pivot column
The system is inconsistent
rooms.explanation: Infinitely many solutions occur when the system is consistent (solvable) AND has at least one free variable (a non-pivot column among the variable columns).

rooms.question: In the augmented matrix [A | b], what condition signals that the system Ax = b is inconsistent?
rooms.latex: [A \mid \mathbf{b}] \text{ row reduces to reveal inconsistency when...}
steps: A row of the form [0\; 0\; \cdots\; 0 \;|\; c] with c \neq 0 means 0 = c, a contradiction.
answers: A pivot appears in the last (augmented) column
A row of all zeros appears
There are more columns than rows
Every row has a pivot
rooms.explanation: If a pivot appears in the augmented column (last column), the row reads 0 = c ≠ 0 — a contradiction — meaning no solution exists.

rooms.question: Order the steps to solve the system x + 2y = 5, 2x + 3y = 8 by row reduction:
items: Back-substitute: from row 2, y=2; substitute into row 1 to get x=1
rooms.explanation: Form the augmented matrix, eliminate below the first pivot, then scale to get a pivot of 1, then back-substitute to find the solution.

boss.name: The Echelon Executioner
boss.taunt: No pivot shall stand in your path — because I will eliminate them all!

questions.question: A 3×5 matrix A (no augmented column) has 3 pivot positions. How many free variables does Ax = 0 have?
questions.explanation: There are 5 variables total and 3 pivots, so the number of free variables = 5 - 3 = 2.

questions.question: After row reducing, you obtain a row [0, 0, 0 | 5] in the augmented matrix. What does this tell you?
answers: The system is inconsistent (no solution)
x₃ = 5
There is a free variable
The system has infinitely many solutions
questions.explanation: The row represents 0x₁ + 0x₂ + 0x₃ = 5, i.e. 0 = 5, which is false. The system is inconsistent.

questions.question: Row operations transform a matrix to RREF. Which statement about the solution set is true?
answers: Row equivalent matrices have the same solution set for Ax=b
Row operations change the solution set
RREF always has a unique solution
Only row swaps preserve the solution set
questions.explanation: All three elementary row operations preserve the solution set of the system. Row equivalent matrices always have identical solution sets.
